name: Sourcegraph / Cody context
description: code search engine and context API serving repo-scale retrieval to agents

developer-org.comment: Founded 2013 by Quinn Slack and Beyang Liu.

implementation-language.comment: Search backend and the Zoekt trigram engine are Go; the Cody clients and web frontend are TypeScript (cody-public-snapshot is 85% TypeScript).

first-release.comment: Merged entity with no single clean date: Sourcegraph code search first released 2013 (exact month unclear), rebranded 'Code Search' in 2023; the Cody AI context layer was unveiled in 2023 and reached GA in December 2023.

status.comment: Sourcegraph Code Search, the MCP server, and Cody Enterprise are actively developed. Cody Free and Pro were discontinued July 2025; individual devs are steered to the newer Amp agent. Checked 2026-07-09.

license.comment: Core platform was Apache-2.0 until June 2023, moved to a proprietary 'Sourcegraph Enterprise' license, and the source repo became private in August 2024. The archived Cody client snapshot remains Apache-2.0 but the living product is proprietary.

open-source.comment: Core repository was made private in August 2024; no longer open source. Only an archived, read-only Cody client snapshot stays Apache-2.0.

repository.comment: Only an archived (read-only since Aug 1, 2025) public snapshot of the Cody client remains; the primary Sourcegraph platform repo went private in August 2024.

github-stars.comment: 3.8k stars on the archived cody-public-snapshot repo (captured 2026-07-09). Not representative of the whole platform, whose core repo is private.

form-factor.comment: Ships as a hosted/self-hosted code-search platform, an MCP server for agents, IDE extensions (Cody for VS Code, JetBrains, Visual Studio), and the src CLI.

standalone-usable.comment: The Sourcegraph MCP server exposes search/navigation/Deep Search to any MCP-capable agent (Claude Code, Cursor, Amp, Codex), so it is not welded to a single host.

bundled-host.comment: Not a bundled engine; it is a standalone platform consumable by many agents.

install-mechanism.comment: Managed cloud (hosted), self-hosted via Docker/Kubernetes/Helm, plus editor extensions for the Cody clients.

retrieval-technique.comment: Core is lexical: Zoekt trigram indexing plus an adapted BM25 ranking. Precise code navigation (go-to-def, references via SCIP/ctags) adds structural retrieval. Hybrid because a single search fuses keyword/regex/semantic signals and Cody globally ranks local + remote results together. Embeddings were used earlier but were deprecated ~2024.

query-interface.comment: Natural-language queries via Cody/Deep Search, symbol lookup via go-to-definition/find-references, keyword/regex lexical search, and MCP tool calls.

returns.comment: Search returns ranked code snippets and file ranges; navigation returns symbols plus find-all-references (call-graph-like); Deep Search returns multi-step investigation summaries.

structural-awareness.comment: Precise code navigation uses SCIP/LSIF indexing and universal-ctags syntactic parsing for go-to-definition and references.

chunking-strategy.comment: The current lexical trigram index does not chunk; retrieval returns file snippets (line ranges), so results are effectively file-level extracts. The deprecated embeddings path previously chunked code.

reranking.comment: Cody applies an adapted BM25 plus 'other signals learned and tuned' and globally re-ranks combined local and remote snippets before packaging the prompt.

index-location.comment: Index lives on a Sourcegraph server, either the managed cloud instance or a self-hosted on-prem deployment; not on the developer's laptop.

incremental-updates.comment: The index server periodically fetches and reindexes repositories from the connected code hosts.

real-time-watch.comment: Auto-syncs by periodically polling and reindexing repositories from the code host, rather than a manual re-index. Not a local file watcher.

vector-store.comment: Cody moved off embeddings to keyword/BM25 search, so there is no vector store in the current retrieval path; earlier versions used an embeddings index.

embedding-model.comment: N/A for the current retrieval path — embeddings (previously OpenAI text-embedding-ada-002) were deprecated in favor of keyword/BM25 search.

mcp-support.comment: Ships a dedicated Sourcegraph MCP server exposing search, navigation, history, and Deep Search; Cody also supports MCP tools via agentic context fetching.

agent-compatibility.comment: MCP server works with MCP-compatible agents including Claude Code, Cursor, Amp, and Codex.

ide-integrations.comment: Cody clients for VS Code, JetBrains, and Visual Studio; the Sourcegraph platform also surfaces via its web UI.

language-agnostic.comment: Trigram/text search indexes any text-based file regardless of language; precise code navigation is limited to the 30+ languages with SCIP/ctags support.

supported-languages.value: Search: any text-based file; precise code navigation supports 30+ languages via SCIP/universal-ctags

code-leaves-machine.comment: On the managed cloud, your code is indexed on Sourcegraph's servers (code egresses). A self-hosted deployment keeps source, user data, and indexes entirely within your own network (false in that mode); AI features still send snippets to LLM providers.

local-only-option.comment: No fully offline laptop-local path: requires a Sourcegraph server (cloud or on-prem), and AI/context features call external LLM providers. Self-hostable on-prem but not air-gapped-local by default.

self-hostable.comment: Enterprise self-hosted deployment runs entirely within your infrastructure via Docker/Kubernetes/Helm; source and indexes never leave your network.

telemetry.comment: Telemetry posture not confirmed in the sources fetched this pass; self-hosted deployments keep data in-network per the self-hosted docs.

max-repo-scale.comment: Explicitly built for very large multi-repo/monorepo estates, indexing every repository into a unified corpus with sub-second queries over billions of lines of code.

pricing-model.comment: Cody Free and Pro were discontinued July 2025, leaving Cody Enterprise ($59/user/mo) and the broader Sourcegraph Enterprise platform (from ~$16K) as paid offerings. Checked 2026-07-09.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cody Enterprise is $59/user/month on an annual contract; the cheapest remaining paid plan after Free/Pro were discontinued. Checked 2026-07-09.

maturity.comment: Stable, widely deployed, enterprise-grade code search platform (a decade+ old) with polished self-hosted tooling.

active-maintenance.comment: Actively developed in 2025 (MCP server, Amp, plan changes), though the core repository is private so commit history is not publicly visible.

community-size.comment: Enterprise-scale adoption; one of the best-known code-search platforms.
